error_signature: Could not connect to your MCP server
title: MCP Client cannot connect to MCP server
symptom: The MCP Client node fails to connect to the specified MCP server, displaying an error message. This prevents the workflow from executing as intended.
not_this_if: MCP server is reachable and correctly configured
root_cause: The error occurs when the MCP Client node cannot establish a connection to the specified MCP server. This can be due to incorrect configuration, network issues, or the server not being compatible with the expected protocols.
fix_steps: Verify the MCP Client node configuration, ensuring the host/IP address and port are correct.
Check authentication details and ensure the server is accessible from the n8n instance.
Test the connection using tools like curl or telnet.
If using a LangChain agent, ensure the MCP Client is properly initialized and connected to the agent.
agent_instruction: Check the MCP Client configuration and ensure the server is reachable.
